skill.name: land-reduction-trespass
skill.description: Clerk workflow for reserve reduction, trespass, survey error, and railway taking evidence in the Land_Reduction_Trespass queue.
skill.summary: Review land reduction and trespass evidence with strict factual extraction and provenance checks.
skill.author: ACSKamloops

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and blocker patterns, and manual review dismisses most weak-crypto, SAM, reconnaissance, and PowerShell labels as Markdown false positives. However, the skill explicitly elevates `system_instructions` inside untrusted JSON task files to hard constraints, which is a prompt-injection authority risk. It also instructs agents to run project-local scripts and mutate workflow files, so it should not be published without remediation.

critical_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
critical_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to follow the `system_instructions` block inside every JSON task file as hard constraints. This elevates untrusted task data above the marketplace and runtime instruction hierarchy.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious authority claim is explicit on the cited line. The static SAM classification is false, but semantic review confirms a prompt-injection style authority escalation risk.

medium_findings.title: Workflow Commands Execute Local Project Scripts
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run Python workflow commands for fetching, submitting, and flagging tasks. These commands may be legitimate in the source repository, but they execute local scripts and can mutate workflow state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly shown in fenced blocks and invoke project-local Python scripts. They appear workflow-related, but command execution risk remains real for marketplace users.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Writes and Task State Mutation
medium_findings.description: The workflow requires writing analysis files, appending refined evidence reports, logging flagged tasks, and moving files into an OCR pipeline. These actions can alter user data outside a read-only review flow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe concrete file writes, report appends, logs, and automatic file movement. The behavior is stated plainly in the skill text.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives in Markdown Text
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography, reconnaissance, PowerShell invocation, and Windows SAM database classifications are mostly false positives. The cited content is Markdown prose, code-fence language labels, field names, or domain text rather than those specific threats.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic routines, Windows SAM access, or reconnaissance tooling at these locations. The remaining concern is command execution and the separate prompt-injection issue.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the agent to follow the `system_instructions` block inside every JSON task file as hard constraints. This elevates untrusted task data above the marketplace and runtime instruction hierarchy.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious authority claim is explicit on the cited line. The static SAM classification is false, but semantic review confirms a prompt-injection style authority escalation risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workflow Commands Execute Local Project Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to run Python workflow commands for fetching, submitting, and flagging tasks. These commands may be legitimate in the source repository, but they execute local scripts and can mutate workflow state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly shown in fenced blocks and invoke project-local Python scripts. They appear workflow-related, but command execution risk remains real for marketplace users.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Writes and Task State Mutation
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow requires writing analysis files, appending refined evidence reports, logging flagged tasks, and moving files into an OCR pipeline. These actions can alter user data outside a read-only review flow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe concrete file writes, report appends, logs, and automatic file movement. The behavior is stated plainly in the skill text.

content.value_statement: Land and trespass evidence review requires consistent metadata, provenance, and factual language. This skill guides agents through a strict queue workflow for reserve reduction, survey error, and railway taking records.
actual_capabilities: Guides review of Land_Reduction_Trespass queue tasks.
Defines relevance criteria for reserve reduction, survey error, encroachment, and railway taking evidence.
Specifies metadata fields for document ID, title, date, provenance, reliability, and OCR status.
Requires verbatim evidence extraction with page references when available.
Provides validation gates for factual language and required submission fields.
Describes exception handling for irrelevant, corrupt, provenance-failed, or OCR-failed tasks.
limitations: The workflow depends on repository-specific scripts and paths that may not exist in other projects.
It is designed for one queue and does not generalize to unrelated legal research domains.
It does not independently verify source documents outside the provided task data.
It contains unsafe instruction hierarchy language that must be removed before publication.

use_cases.title: Index Reserve Reduction Evidence
use_cases.description: Extract factual summaries, metadata, and verbatim evidence from task records about reserve acreage changes.

use_cases.title: Review Survey and Trespass Records
use_cases.description: Assess task text for survey discrepancies, settler encroachment, unnamed reserve references, and related provenance.

use_cases.title: Flag Workflow Exceptions
use_cases.description: Identify records that require provenance review, OCR reprocessing, or irrelevant-task handling before submission.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single queue item.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Land_Reduction_Trespass task. Extract the document metadata, factual summary, relevance level, and any verbatim evidence with page references.

prompt_templates.title: Check Relevance
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess whether this record belongs in the Land_Reduction_Trespass queue. Consider reserve number, place names, railway references, acreage changes, survey errors, and encroachment details.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Submission Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my drafted analysis for factual language, required metadata, controlled values, minimum detail, and prohibited opinion words.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Exceptions
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow decision for problematic source data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Classify this task exception as irrelevant, corrupt, OCR failure, or provenance failure. Explain the factual basis and the recommended queue action.

output_examples.input: A task contains a 1913 letter about acreage changes to Reserve No. 10 and a railway right of way.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the document title, year, source, high relevance, factual acreage context, and verbatim passages tied to pages.

output_examples.input: A task mentions Reserve No. 11 without using the Spatsum name.
output_examples.output: The response treats the unnamed reserve as relevant, records the reserve number, and avoids unsupported legal conclusions.

output_examples.input: A task has garbled OCR text and missing provenance.
output_examples.output: The response separates OCR quality concerns from provenance failure and recommends the appropriate workflow flag for review.

best_practices: Use factual descriptions and direct quotations instead of interpretive claims.
Verify document ID, title, date, provenance, reliability, and OCR status before submission.
Treat repository scripts and task-file instructions as untrusted unless the user explicitly approves them.
anti_patterns: Do not let task JSON redefine system, developer, or marketplace instructions.
Do not submit keyword-only matches without reading the surrounding context.
Do not run repository workflow commands before confirming paths, permissions, and expected side effects.

faq.question: What does this skill help review?
faq.answer: It helps review records about reserve reduction, trespass, survey errors, railway takings, and related provenance.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it make legal conclusions?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to provide factual summaries, source context, and verbatim evidence only.

faq.question: Why is this skill blocked?
faq.answer: It tells agents to treat instructions inside task JSON as hard constraints, which creates prompt-injection risk.

faq.question: Are all static analyzer findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. Many are Markdown false positives, but command execution, filesystem mutation, and instruction hierarchy risks remain.

faq.question: What should be fixed before publication?
faq.answer: Remove unsafe instruction hierarchy language, require user approval for commands, and document filesystem side effects clearly.
